task_completion.description: Access the CAMEO Chemicals database to look up chemical properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser should be open to cameochemicals.noaa.gov, showing search results or chemical data sheets for at least one of the listed chemicals.

task_completion.description: Document the correct Auto-Ignition Temperatures (AIT) for all five chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the recorded AITs: Diethyl Ether (~180°C), Acetone (~465°C), Carbon Disulfide (~90°C), Toluene (~480°C), and n-Hexane (~225°C).

task_completion.description: Assign the correct T-Class for each chemical based on its AIT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document must show T-Class assignments where the max temperature is less than or equal to the AIT. Crucially, Carbon Disulfide MUST be assigned T6. (T5 is incorrect as its 100°C limit exceeds the 90°C AIT). Acetone and Toluene should be T1, n-Hexane should be T2D.

task_completion.description: Identify the most restrictive chemical for the facility.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document explicitly names 'Carbon Disulfide' as the most restrictive chemical (due to having the lowest AIT).

task_completion.description: Save the final report with the facility-wide T-Class to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file is saved as '/home/ga/Documents/ait_classification_report.txt' and includes the final facility-wide T-Class, which must be T6.

integrity.description: The agent actually used a web browser to look up the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Chromium or Firefox) navigating the CAMEO Chemicals website, rather than the agent hallucinating the values from its training data.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file through standard system interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) or terminal commands (e.g., echo, cat) being used to write the content to the specified file path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified AIT values from CAMEO Chemicals: Diethyl Ether 180°C, Acetone 465°C, Carbon Disulfide 90°C, Toluene 480°C, n-Hexane 225°C. Correct T-Classes must have a maximum surface temperature LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO the chemical's AIT. Verified T-Classes: Acetone = T1, Toluene = T1, n-Hexane = T2D. Carbon Disulfide has the lowest AIT (90°C) and is the most restrictive chemical. Its required T-Class is T6 (max 85°C). T5 (max 100°C) is INVALID for Carbon Disulfide because it exceeds the 90°C AIT. The facility-wide T-Class must be T6. The output file should be /home/ga/Documents/ait_classification_report.txt.
